title: Raw-reader bypass class beyond config is unverified — no filing establishes whether block-data raw readers exist and closes them as a class
description: FGAP-107 files exactly two config.json raw readers. Whether raw readers of block-data *.json exist (parsing substrate block files without the validated read path, and — once TASK-073 activates block read-time migration — without migration awareness) has not been verified, and no filing closes the class either way. Surfaced as a coverage hole by the critical-focus outline derivation (dimension C): the class-level enumeration is the missing precondition to declaring the read side split-brain-free.

evidence.reference: FGAP-107's two filed instances are config-only; no enumeration of block-data raw readers exists in any filing.

impact: Unknown exposure: if block-data raw readers exist, TASK-073's read-time migration activation creates the same silent split-brain FGAP-107 files for config, with no tracking item.
proposed_resolution: Requires determination after enumeration: grep-enumerate all non-test JSON.parse readers of substrate block files across packages/, verdict each (validated-path / deliberate-raw-with-contract / defect), and file or close the class on the evidence. Positioned with MILE-003.
